title: Smoky Gooey Filipino White Yam Curry
description: A fresh, smoky curry made with tender white yam and coconut milk, perfect for a lowsodium postworkout dinner.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and ginger, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the smoked paprika and chili flakes, cooking for another 30 seconds to bloom the spices.
Add the diced white yam to the pan and stir to coat the pieces with oil and spices.
Pour in the coconut milk, low‑sodium soy sauce, and low‑sodium fish sauce; stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it cook for 15 minutes or until the yam is tender and the curry thickens slightly.
Uncover, stir in the lime juice, and taste for seasoning—adjust with a pinch of salt if needed.
Continue to simmer uncovered for 5 minutes so the sauce becomes thicker and the yam turns gooey.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
